  • 1) Strain abundance vs. number of mutations per Mb
    • 1.1) Scatterplot by different treatment conditions

Metagenome Analysis

Only ancestral, streptomycin and control treatments are included, unless otherwise stated.

Authors

Niina Smolander

Shane Hogle

Libraries and functions

Prior to this analysis, the variants have been filtered using Mutect2’s FilterMutectCalls as well as separately for SNPs and INDELs using the following hard filtering factors: SOR, FS, MQ, MQRankSum, ReadPosRankSum, SNP/INDEL clustering (3/35) and SNP/INDEL proximity.

Code 
library(tidyverse)
library(GenomicRanges)

1) Strain abundance vs. number of mutations per Mb

Code 
# these are strains with max freq of 0.005 at t0 and t3 and contain no variants.
lowstrainsv <- c(
  "HAMBI_0097",
  "HAMBI_0262",
  "HAMBI_1988",
  "HAMBI_2159",
  "HAMBI_2494",
  "HAMBI_2792",
  "HAMBI_3237"
)

# variants_strep <- read_rds("data/input/variants_strep.rds") %>%
#   filter(!str_detect(EFFECT, "intergenic|intragenic|synonymous|fusion")) %>%
#   filter(!str_detect(IMPACT, "MODIFIER")) %>%
#   filter(!(HAMBI %in% lowstrainsv))


variants_strep <- read_rds("data/input/variants_strep.rds") %>%
  filter(!(HAMBI %in% lowstrainsv))


# MGE
genomad <- read_tsv("data/input/MGE/genomad_HAMBI_combined.tsv",
                    skip = 1,
                    col_names = c("HAMBI",
                                  "CHROM",
                                  "Start",
                                  "End",
                                  "Name",
                                  "prediction_tool"),
                    col_types = c("ccddcc"))

mgefinder <- read_tsv("data/input/MGE/MGE_finder_HAMBI_combined.tsv",
                      skip = 1,
                      col_names = c("HAMBI",
                                    "Name",
                                    "CHROM",
                                    "Start",
                                    "End",
                                    "prediction_tool"),
                      col_types = c("cccddc"))


#chrom lengths
chrom_lengths <- read_tsv("data/input/chrom_lengths.tsv")

full_mge <- bind_rows(select(genomad, CHROM, Start, End),
                      select(mgefinder, CHROM, Start, End)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(seqnames = CHROM) %>%
  mutate(IRanges = paste(Start, End, sep = "-")) %>%
  select(-Start, -End)

mge_gr <- GenomicRanges::GRanges(seqnames = full_mge$seqnames,
                  ranges = full_mge$IRanges)


mge_gr_reduced <- GenomicRanges::reduce(mge_gr) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(HAMBI = substring(seqnames, 1, 10)) %>%
  group_by(HAMBI) %>%
  mutate(mge_length = sum(width)) %>%
  ungroup()

chrom_lengths <- left_join(chrom_lengths,
                           distinct(select(mge_gr_reduced, HAMBI, mge_length))) %>%
  mutate(mge_length = ifelse(is.na(mge_length),
                             0,
                             mge_length),
         new_length = sum_bases - mge_length)


n_mut <- variants_strep %>%
  group_by(HAMBI, Sample) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(chrom_lengths, by = join_by(HAMBI)) %>%
  mutate(mut_per_Mb = n/(new_length/1000000)) %>%
  left_join(distinct(select(variants_strep, Sample, measure_env, home_env, replicate)),
            by = join_by(Sample)) %>%
  select(Sample, home_env, measure_env, replicate, HAMBI, n, mut_per_Mb) %>%
  distinct()


hambi_colors <- c(`HAMBI_1287` = "#6DA3EE", `HAMBI_1977` = "#F1C82E", `HAMBI_0403` = "#FB8A5C", 
                  `HAMBI_2659` = "#624090", `HAMBI_1972` = "#26818E", `HAMBI_1292` = "#32702C", 
                  `HAMBI_1923` = "#FC2EDB", `HAMBI_1279` = "#FE1C35", `HAMBI_1299` = "#BEEF60", 
                  `HAMBI_1896` = "#1C26FB", `HAMBI_0097` = "#F0DACB", `HAMBI_1988` = "#D71C76", 
                  `HAMBI_0006` = "#870DAE", `HAMBI_2792` = "#0DFE32", `HAMBI_3031` = "#FC94D1", 
                  `HAMBI_2160` = "#16EEA5", `HAMBI_0105` = "#00F5F7", `HAMBI_3237` = "#956616", 
                  `HAMBI_2494` = "#DABBF3", `HAMBI_2164` = "#DF26FD", `HAMBI_0262` = "#8D324F", 
                  `HAMBI_2443` = "#9DE0C5", `HAMBI_2159` = "#92950D", `HAMBI_1842` = "#F489FD"
)



hambi_colors2 <- as.data.frame(hambi_colors) %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "strainID")


sptable <- readr::read_tsv("data/input/species_counts_md.tsv") %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(transfer = day/7,
                strainID2 = paste0(substring(genus, 1,1), ". ", species)) %>%
  left_join(hambi_colors2)

counts_f <- sptable %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(sample) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(f=count_correct/sum(count_correct)) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(measure_env_short = dplyr::case_when(measure_env == "bact" ~ "Meas: B",
                                                     measure_env == "bact_strep" ~ "Meas: BS"),
                measure_env_short = ifelse(is.na(measure_env_short),
                                           "t0",
                                           measure_env_short)) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(home_env_short = dplyr::case_when(
    evolution_env == "anc" ~ "Home: Anc",
    evolution_env == "bact" ~ "Home: B",
    evolution_env == "bact_strep" ~ "Home: BS")) %>% 
  # dplyr::mutate(
  #   measure_env_short = factor(measure_env_short, levels = c("Meas: B", "Meas: BS")),
  #   home_env_short = factor(home_env_short, levels = c("Home: Anc", "Home: B", "Home: BS")),
  #   day = factor(day),
  #   replicate = factor(replicate)) %>%
  select(sample, measure_env_short, home_env_short, replicate, strainID, f, strainID2, hambi_colors) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  filter(sample %in% n_mut$Sample) %>%
  left_join(select(n_mut, Sample, HAMBI, mut_per_Mb), by = join_by(sample == Sample, strainID == HAMBI)) %>%
  mutate(mut_per_Mb = ifelse(is.na(mut_per_Mb),
                             0,
                             mut_per_Mb),
         treatment = as.factor(paste(home_env_short, measure_env_short, sep = " ; "))) %>%
  filter(!(strainID %in% lowstrainsv))

p <- counts_f %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = mut_per_Mb, x = f, color = strainID2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +
  facet_grid(home_env_short ~ measure_env_short)  +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(counts_f$hambi_colors, counts_f$strainID2))

p2 <- counts_f %>%
  group_by(strainID) %>%
  mutate(max = max(mut_per_Mb)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(max > 0) %>%
  ggplot(aes(f, mut_per_Mb, color = treatment)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(strainID2 ~ ., scales = "free") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("purple", "purple4",
                                "orange", "orange3", "brown",
                                "cyan", "lightblue", "blue"))

1.1) Scatterplot by different treatment conditions

Comparing the strain abundance and mutations per Mb. Particularly in the t0 samples, “medium” abundant strains have accumulated the most mutations

Code 
p

1.2) Scatterplot by different strains

Comparing the strain abundance and mutations per Mb. Different strains have different scales and strains with no mutations have been filtered out.

Code 
p2
